screenshot_note: Desktop 1280x800 and mobile 375x812 captured via Chromium headless. Both verified. Sofia's lone-survivor label visually bleeds outside office-floor left edge on desktop. Speech bubble text confirmed overflowing office-floor container on mobile. Bloberto card obscures most of the office floor on narrow viewports.

carry_over_resolution.1_resume_button_noop: ❌ STILL BROKEN — App.jsx:1211 onResume is still `() => setPollingPaused(false)`. The idle-check interval at line 719-726 runs every 15 seconds and calls `setPollingPaused(Date.now() - lastActivity.current > IDLE_TIMEOUT)`. Since lastActivity.current is never updated on Resume, the button re-pauses within 15 seconds of clicking. Fix requires `lastActivity.current = Date.now()` inside onResume. This is now broken across MULTIPLE reviews — escalating to CRITICAL.
carry_over_resolution.2_pingReaction_only_bloberto: ❌ STILL BROKEN — Office.jsx line 1439 is the only Character that receives pingReaction={pingReaction}. Worker characters at desks (line 1453-1455) and idle wanderers (lines 1473-1483) do NOT receive the pingReaction prop. PING_REACTIONS has entries for luna, carlos, maya, dave, sofia, but clicking any of them only produces the pingedId pulse highlight — the speech bubble never fires. Escalating.
carry_over_resolution.3_speech_bubble_mobile_overflow: ❌ STILL BROKEN — Office.css line 84: .office-floor { overflow: visible }. Confirmed visually in mobile screenshot: the 'bugs have nowhere to hide' text and Sofia's speech bubble text clip/bleed outside the office-floor container boundary. No @media override exists to constrain it. Escalating.
carry_over_resolution.4_wb_task_count_wraps_mobile: ❌ STILL BROKEN — Office.jsx line 1267: wb-task-count span has inline style only (fontSize: '0.4rem'). No white-space: nowrap anywhere. No dedicated CSS class rule exists for .wb-task-count. Text '2 active · 5 total' wraps at narrow widths. Escalating.
carry_over_resolution.5_useEffect_dep_missing_worker_id: ❌ STILL BROKEN — Office.jsx line 534-550: error bubble useEffect dep array is [variant, worker.status] but the closure captures worker.id (line 538: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES[worker.id]) and wanderIdx (line 548: wanderIdx * 1500). Stale closure risk when worker identity changes mid-render. Also affects idle bubble useEffect at line 511-523 which uses wanderIdx correctly in deps but the error one doesn't. Escalating.

suggestions.title: [ESCALATED #4] Resume button re-pauses itself within 15s
suggestions.description: App.jsx:1211 — onResume callback only calls setPollingPaused(false) but never resets lastActivity.current. The idle-check interval at line 719-726 fires every 15 seconds and re-evaluates `Date.now() - lastActivity.current > IDLE_TIMEOUT`, which immediately re-pauses since lastActivity.current was never touched. Fix: `onResume={() => { lastActivity.current = Date.now(); setPollingPaused(false) }}`. This has been flagged in reviews 38, 39, 40, and 41 without a fix.

suggestions.title: [ESCALATED #4] pingReaction silently dropped for all non-Bloberto workers
suggestions.description: Office.jsx — handleCharClick (line 1125-1136) correctly sets pingReaction state from PING_REACTIONS[worker.id], and PING_REACTIONS has entries for all five workers. However, the pingReaction prop is only passed to the single Bloberto Character at line 1439. All desk-worker Characters (line 1447-1455) and idle Characters (line 1473-1483) are missing `pingReaction={pingedId === w.id ? PING_REACTIONS[w.id] : null}`. The state is set, the data exists, the wiring is just absent. Fix: pass the reaction through to each Character, scoped to that worker's id.

suggestions.title: [ESCALATED #4] Speech bubble overflows office-floor on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: .office-floor { overflow: visible }. Confirmed in mobile screenshot — speech bubble text bleeds outside the container boundary. The .speech-bubble is positioned absolute with `bottom: calc(100% + 8px)`, which places it above its parent. When the parent is near the top of the office-floor and the floor has overflow:visible, the bubble clips out. Fix options: (1) add `overflow: hidden` to .office-floor with a conditional for the top region, or (2) set a clip-path on the character container, or (3) use a portal to render speech bubbles in a higher z-index layer outside the floor.

suggestions.title: [ESCALATED #4] .wb-task-count wraps on mobile — still no white-space:nowrap
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1267: the wb-task-count span has only `style={{ fontSize: '0.4rem', color: 'inherit', opacity: 0.7 }}`. There is no CSS class rule for .wb-task-count and no white-space:nowrap. At narrow viewport widths the text '2 active · 5 total' wraps to two lines, breaking whiteboard layout. One-line fix: add `whiteSpace: 'nowrap'` to the inline style, or add a .wb-task-count { white-space: nowrap; } rule to Office.css.

suggestions.title: [ESCALATED #4] Error bubble useEffect dep array missing worker.id and wanderIdx
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 534-550: the error-bubble useEffect uses `WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES[worker.id]` (line 538) and `wanderIdx * 1500` (line 548) in its body but neither is listed in the dep array `[variant, worker.status]`. If worker.id changes (roster reload reassigns a different worker to the same Character slot) or wanderIdx changes, the closure retains stale values and shows the wrong worker's bubbles at the wrong timing. Fix: change dep array to `[variant, worker.status, worker.id, wanderIdx]`.

suggestions.title: Always-true guard in desk monitor rendering (|| true anti-pattern)
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1361: `(isWorking || hasError) && (DESK_SCREEN_SVG[occ.worker.id] || true)`. The `|| true` makes the second condition always truthy, effectively making it equivalent to just `(isWorking || hasError)`. The intent was probably to render a fallback monitor even when no SVG entry exists, but the current code always renders the outer wrapper div regardless. This is misleading dead code. Remove the `|| true` and let the inner check for `DESK_SCREEN_SVG[...]` handle the fallback SVG display.

suggestions.title: No feedback after Resume — user has no confirmation refresh restarted
suggestions.description: Even after the Resume button is fixed to update lastActivity.current, there is still no visual confirmation that polling resumed. The sync-status card flips from PAUSED back to Live, but there is no transient toast or animation to confirm the action. Consider a brief 'Resuming...' flash on the stat card or a short 'refresh resumed' toast notification to close the interaction loop.

suggestions.title: stuckWorkers useMemo uses Date.now() without declaring it as dependency
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 438-440: `useMemo(() => workers.filter(w => ... Date.now() ... > 7_200_000), [workers])`. The Date.now() call is baked in but not a dependency, so if a worker crosses the 2-hour stuck threshold between worker array refreshes, the memo won't recompute. In practice polling every 30s limits the window, but it's a correctness issue. Fix: compute the threshold outside the memo (e.g., a useTimeTick-based value) or just run it as a plain derived value without memoization since workers.filter is cheap.

suggestions.title: IIFE inside JSX for solo spotlight message — unnecessary per-render allocation
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1239: `{(() => { const soloKey = ...; return SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES[soloKey]?.[teamVibe.key] ?? ... })()}`. An immediately-invoked function expression inside JSX runs on every render. Since activeWorkers[0] and teamVibe.key are already in scope, this logic should be extracted to a local variable before the return statement or a useMemo, not an inline IIFE.

suggestions.title: ActivityLogEntry key uses array index after reverse/slice — fragile reconciliation
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 151: `key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i}` where `i` comes from indexing into `.reverse().slice(0, 20)`. The index is volatile: adding a new entry changes every i-keyed item below it, forcing React to remount all but the first element. If entry.timestamp is unique (which it should be for activity events), just use `key={entry.timestamp + (entry.worker ?? '')}` and drop the index.

suggestions.title: Pingable worker Characters lack descriptive interactive label
suggestions.description: Office.jsx — Characters with onClick handlers (desk workers line 1453, idle wanderers line 1480) are clickable but have no `aria-label` describing the action. Screen reader users see a generic element with no hint that clicking pings the worker. Add `aria-label={`Ping ${worker.name}`}` to the Character component when onClick is present, and `role='button'` with `tabIndex={0}` if it's a non-button element.

suggestions.title: ProgressBar 'updatedAt' prop accepted but never used
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 193: `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })` — updatedAt is destructured but never referenced anywhere in the function body. It was likely superseded by startedAt for the ETA calculation. Remove it from the signature and from the two call sites (lines 346 and similar) to keep the interface clean.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline has redundant aria-label + <title> with identical content
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 401-402: the SVG element has both `aria-label={history.map(...).join(' then ')}` and a child `<title>` with the identical string. When both are present, screen readers may announce the content twice. Remove the `aria-label` from the SVG and keep the `<title>` child, which is the SVG-native accessibility mechanism.

suggestions.title: Mobile: Bloberto's hover-card obscures most of the office floor
suggestions.description: Visible in the mobile screenshot — Bloberto's expanded info card (tooltip) at top-center covers roughly 40% of the office-floor vertical space on a 375px viewport. The card appears to be absolutely positioned without a mobile max-height or overflow constraint. Consider capping the card height on mobile or pinning it outside the office-floor container so workers below remain visible.

suggestions.title: Desktop: Sofia's lone-survivor label clips outside office-floor left edge
suggestions.description: Visible in the desktop screenshot — the lone-survivor label for Sofia (leftmost desk) uses `left: '50%', transform: 'translateX(-50%)'` which centers it on the character, but when the character is at the far-left desk the label's left half bleeds outside the office-floor container. The container's overflow:visible allows this. Consider adding `max-width: 90px; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis` to the lone-survivor-label inline style, or shifting the label right for edge desks.

summary: Review #41 — all five carry-over HIGH items remain unaddressed for the fourth consecutive review. The Resume button, pingReaction wiring, speech bubble overflow, wb-task-count wrap, and useEffect dep array are fully reproducible and trivially fixable — most are one-line changes. A new always-true guard (`|| true`) was spotted in the desk rendering path. Visually the app looks genuinely polished at desktop; mobile is functional but cramped. Fun factor remains high — Bloberto's ambient quotes and the vibe-aware character expressions are genuinely charming. But charm doesn't fix a Resume button that un-resumes itself. Fix the carry-overs first. Everything else is gravy.
